title: Health Canada and JTI-Macdonald meeting: Approach to and perspectives on Emerging Products – October 24, 2017 - Canada.ca
content:
## Health Canada and JTI-Macdonald meeting: Approach to and perspectives on Emerging Products – October 24, 2017
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 78 KB, 2 pages)
JTI’s approach to and  perspectives on Emerging Products
- James Van Loon Director General, Tobacco  Control Directorate (TCD) (Chair)
- Director General, Tobacco  Control Directorate (TCD) (Chair)
- Denis Choinière Director, Tobacco Products  Regulatory Office (TCD)
- Director, Tobacco Products  Regulatory Office (TCD)
- Dana Beaton Acting Director, Office of  Research and Surveillance (TCD)
- Acting Director, Office of  Research and Surveillance (TCD)
- Saira David Director, Labelling and Plain  Packaging Office (TCD)
- Director, Labelling and Plain  Packaging Office (TCD)
- Manager, Regulations  Division, Tobacco Products Regulatory Office (TCD)
- Manager, Reports Control  Division, Tobacco Products Regulatory Office (TCD)
- Regulatory Specialist, Labelling  and Plain Packaging Office (TCD)
- Advisor, Director General  Office (TCD)

### JTI-Macdonald Corp. (JTI)
- Caroline Evans Head of Corporate Affairs  & Communication (JTI)
- Head of Corporate Affairs  & Communication (JTI)
- Ian  Jones Vice  President, Reduced Risk Products (JTI)
- Vice  President, Reduced Risk Products (JTI)
- Leticia  Vazquez Santos Manager,  Reduced Risk Products Regulatory Initiatives (JTI)
- Manager,  Reduced Risk Products Regulatory Initiatives (JTI)
- Shandy  Harricharran Manager,  Scientific Regulatory Affairs (JTI)
- Manager,  Scientific Regulatory Affairs (JTI)
This meeting was held at the request of JTI so that  the company could present its approach  to and perspectives on Emerging Products.
The Chair opened the meeting by doing round table  introductions.
HC reiterated its international obligation to protect  tobacco control policies from the vested interests of the tobacco industry. In  addition, in the interest of transparency, the department stated that it would  be making a record of the meeting publicly available.
JTI presented its Ploom TECH hybrid product. It comprises a  capsule with liquid that is vapourized and which then goes through granulated  tobacco kept in the cap. The product comes in a small number of flavours.
The company representatives feel that reduced-risk products  are the next wave of tobacco products. They also indicated that data on their  product is available on the company’s science website (https://www.jt-science.com).
JTI representatives said they were looking at bringing Ploom  TECH to the Canadian market in the next few months.
The meeting was then concluded.
